# 🛡️ Code of Conduct

## 📌 Purpose

This project aims to keep a respectful, useful, and collaborative environment for everyone involved.

Whether you are opening an issue, submitting a pull request, reviewing code, or discussing changes, you are expected to act in a way that helps the project and respects other people.

## 🌍 Scope

This Code of Conduct applies to:

- this GitHub repository
- issues
- pull requests
- discussions in project-related threads
- code reviews
- other public interactions directly connected to this repository

This policy applies to maintainers, contributors, and community members alike.

## ✅ Expected Behavior

Examples of positive behavior include:

- 🤝 being respectful and constructive
- 🧠 discussing technical topics in good faith
- 🛠️ helping others solve problems without hostility
- 💬 giving feedback clearly and professionally
- 🙌 accepting corrections and learning from mistakes
- ⏳ being patient with maintainers and contributors
- 📄 reporting bugs with useful details instead of blame

## ❌ Unacceptable Behavior

Examples of unacceptable behavior include:

- 🚫 harassment, bullying, or intimidation
- 🚫 personal attacks, insults, or repeated hostility
- 🚫 hate speech or discriminatory behavior
- 🚫 doxxing or sharing private information without permission
- 🚫 spam, trolling, or intentionally disruptive behavior
- 🚫 aggressive demands for support or updates
- 🚫 misleading, dishonest, or bad-faith issue reports
- 🚫 using this repository to attack, target, or harass other projects or people

## 🧩 Project-Specific Expectations

Because this is an **unofficial Slimefun fork**, contributors should also follow these expectations:

- ❤️ keep proper credit to the original Slimefun project
- 🔗 do not misrepresent this fork as the official upstream project
- 🐞 report issues for this build to this repository, not the official Slimefun project
- 🛠️ keep contributions relevant to this fork's scope, especially compatibility and stability work
- 📦 avoid breaking packaging, licensing, attribution, or release information

## ⚖️ Enforcement

Project maintainers may take action if behavior violates this Code of Conduct.

Possible actions include:

- warning the user
- editing or removing comments
- closing issues or pull requests
- temporarily limiting participation
- permanently blocking users from the repository

Moderation decisions will be made based on what best protects the project and its contributors.

## 📬 Reporting

If you experience or witness unacceptable behavior, report it to the repository maintainer through GitHub in a responsible and private way when possible.

When reporting:

- include links or screenshots if available
- explain what happened clearly
- avoid escalating the issue publicly unless necessary

Reports should be handled fairly and respectfully.

## 🙏 Respect for Upstream

This repository is based on the original Slimefun project created by **[TheBusyBiscuit](https://github.com/TheBusyBiscuit)** and the **Slimefun Team**.

Please respect the original authors and do not redirect blame, harassment, or unofficial bug reports toward the official upstream maintainers.

## 📖 Attribution

This Code of Conduct is inspired by the general principles of open-source community standards and the Contributor Covenant, adapted for this repository and its unofficial fork status.
